what_went_right: Mandatory code-reviewer ran before push and surfaced 3 Should-fixes (0 Critical); ALL three were folded in before the commit landed — none dismissed. The headline catch (the double-scroll burying the chart on the exact chip-click path the feature exists for) was a real behavioral bug invisible to the pure unit tests.

The new pure logic (extractQueryTickers in lib/trending.js) shipped with 16 dedicated tests covering the trending-chip bare-symbol case, $-cashtags, multi-token all-caps, lowercase-KNOWN-ticker, stray-capitalized-prose rejection, command/author filters, dedup, the 6-symbol cap (explicitly asserting the 7th is DROPPED not silently truncated), and empty/non-string input. Full suite 529/529 green, run before push.

Iframe lifecycle was designed for the right cost model up front: _chartPaneKey caches the (theme|symbols) signature so the heavy TradingView iframes rebuild ONLY when the symbol set or theme changes — never on the per-keystroke / per-poll applySearch re-renders that would otherwise flicker. The chartPane lives inside #stream but OUTSIDE #messages so renderMessages()'s rebuild never touches it, and the top-snap fires exactly once (chartJustShown) so polling never yanks the scroll.

The trending-noise fix was correctly defense-in-depth: URLs are stripped before topic extraction (URL_RE handles both scheme/www and bare host.tld forms) AND the scheme/host fragments were added to TOPIC_STOPWORDS as belt-and-suspenders, with 3 new tests proving https/www/com/host-words/bare-domains don't trend.

extractQueryTickers explicitly rejects slashless key:value commands (from:elon, has:image, since:3) via the /^\w+:/ guard rather than relying on them happening to fail the downstream symbol checks — the reviewer's second Should-fix, fixed by making the doc-comment contract actually true.
what_went_wrong: Three live UI-sizing corrections were needed post-first-cut: card stretched full-row -> capped, then 'still smaller, maybe 2/3rds size'; and the ⤢ expand icon overlapped TradingView's own top-right logo and had to be moved (now pinned bottom-right). The first cut did not front-load chart-card geometry / control-overlap despite this being a known recurring BetterSSC failure mode for UI the agent can't drive live.

Version state was discovered stale at commit time — package.json was at 0.6.0 while CHANGELOG/tags were at 0.7.1 — and had to be reconciled to 0.8.0 mid-ship. A lingering version-drift smell that surfaced as cleanup work rather than being caught earlier.
three_whys: Why 1: The chip-click ticker search momentarily showed the chart then immediately scrolled it out of view (caught as a code-reviewer Should-fix, not in the live demo).

Why 2: applySearch was updated for the new ticker-query behavior (focusSearchHit with scroll:false + a one-time stream.scrollTop=0 to pin the chart), but the caller searchForTerm STILL called focusSearchHit unconditionally afterward — and that later, scroll:true call won, overriding the helper's just-set intent.

Why 3: When a shared helper both sets state and performs a side-effect (scroll/focus), adding a scroll:false opt-out to the helper is necessary but not sufficient — every caller that independently drives the SAME side-effect must also be audited and guarded, because the side-effect that fires LAST silently wins. The fix only adding the opt-out to the helper without grepping callers would have left the bug live.

drift_checklist.finding: Three live UI-sizing/placement corrections on the chart cards: (1) cards stretched full-row, user asked them capped to a fixed size with empty space rather than ballooning; (2) ⤢ maximize icon overlapped TradingView's own logo, user said 'put them in separate locations'; (3) 'still smaller, maybe 2/3rds size'. These are functionality-accepted, geometry-iterated — re-confirms the existing live-eyes memory rather than a new class.

citations.quote: i like the intraday charts - but they shoudl always the sized to this size - when there are 3 tickres, they are the right size. so maybe even with 1 ticker, you leave empty space but have it sized no more than what you see in this screenshot. secondly your ticker maximize icon is overlaying the TV icon and none of them is distinguishable. you need to put them in separate locations.
i think the chart needs to be still smaller. maybe 2/3rds size of what you are showing now.

evidence_citations.quote: app.js:1649 — Double-scroll on trending-chip click with ticker query. ... For a ticker query, applySearch already ran focusSearchHit(0, { scroll: false }) and set stream.scrollTop = 0. The second focusSearchHit call at line 1649 passes no opts -> opts.scroll !== false -> scrollIntoView(...) fires, scrolling the viewport from the chart-pane top to the first message hit. The chart pane the user was supposed to see is immediately buried.
function focusSearchHit(idx, opts = {}) { ... if (opts.scroll !== false) { node.scrollIntoView({ behavior: "smooth", block: "center" }); }

action_items.title: Front-load chart-card geometry + control-overlap into the FIRST cut for any embedded-widget UI
action_items.description: This ship needed 3 live corrections (full-width->capped->2/3 size; ⤢ overlapping TradingView's own logo). This re-confirms ui-affordance-needs-live-eyes-not-headless-dom-presence rather than a new class. When embedding a third-party widget (TradingView mini-chart, etc.) the agent can't drive live: in the first cut, (a) cap the card to a fixed max-width so 1-2 instances don't balloon, (b) inventory the widget's OWN chrome (TradingView pins its logo top-right) and place any added controls in a non-colliding corner, (c) stage ONE live-eyes checklist for size/overlap. Cite the existing memory; do not write a duplicate.
